entry.title: How is the lifecycle status calculated in Cloud ALM - Landscape Management
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP CALM Landscape management: some system shows status as active and some as new.

How is the lifecycle status calculated?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How is the lifecycle status calculated in Cloud ALM -...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Active:

  • Configuration exists AND
  • Data Collection is activated

Inactive: 

  • Deactivation of a use case in the managed system
  • Configuration exists AND
  • Data collection is disabled AND
  • Old data can exist (but might have been cleaned up by housekeeping) 


Not configured:

  • Deregistration of the entire managed service/system AND
  • No configuration exists
  • Note: During the deregistration/configuration removal there should be a pop-up saying "If you remove the configuration, you will also remove existing data for the managed service/system. Are you sure? If you want to keep the data, consider only deactivating the data collection for the service/system"
  • NEW would fall under not configured too

Best regards,
Ellie

entry.title: Re: Relaxed Flow not working in Work Center POD
entry.content:
Hi Manoel,

Thank you for looking into this. 

In our case, we have changed the Relaxed Flow as "True" and we have SFC with quantity as 1 only. 

We have 3 operations 10, 20 and 30 for the same SFC and user wants to start any of the operation at any time. 

Like - They can start/complete the 20 operations, before starting the operation 10. 

But even changing the Relaxed Flow as "True", it is not allowing to start the 20 operations before starting the 10 operations. 

Hope I am able to explain. 

Note - We tried using Work Center POD, Operation Activities POD and also using the Production process to start the operation. But nothing is working.  

Thanks,

Nikhil Gupta

entry.title: Re: Relaxed Flow not working in Work Center POD
entry.content:
Your understanding of the "Relaxed Flow" setting is correct in principle; however, there are a few key aspects of its configuration and dependencies that could explain why you are unable to start the second operation. Let’s investigate the potential reasons:

1. Predecessor Relationships in the Routing

  • Even with Relaxed Flow set to true, operations in the routing may still have predecessor-successor dependencies configured. For instance, the second operation might have a constraint requiring the first operation to be completed or at least started before it can begin.
  • Solution: Check the routing's operation dependencies and adjust the precedence settings if necessary.

2. Control Key Settings

  • The control key assigned to the operations might enforce strict sequential processing despite the relaxed flow setting. For example, control keys with dependencies for "Setup," "Start," or "Finish" could prevent starting operations out of order.
  • Solution: Verify and update the control key configurations to allow more flexible operation starts.

3. System Configuration for Relaxed Flow

  • Ensure that Relaxed Flow = True is properly activated and configured for the production order. Sometimes, incorrect or partial activation in the system settings can cause unexpected behavior.
  • Solution: Double-check the production order parameters and the system configuration to ensure they align with the intended relaxed flow functionality.

4. Operation Confirmation or Status Issues

  • The second operation might have a prerequisite status or a dependency on the first operation’s confirmation or progress. If the first operation hasn’t been partially or fully confirmed, the system could block the second one from starting.
  • Solution: Check the status and confirmation settings for the operations.

5. Capacity Constraints

  • If the second operation requires specific resources (e.g., machines or labor), and those are not available or allocated to the first operation, it could prevent the second operation from starting.
  • Solution: Review the resource allocation and capacity planning to ensure the required resources are available.

6. Custom Enhancements or Restrictions

  • If your system has any custom logic or user exits in place, there might be additional restrictions applied beyond the standard relaxed flow functionality.
  • Solution: Collaborate with your technical team to review any customizations impacting the production order processing.

Next Steps

  1. Validate the routing and control key configurations.
  2. Ensure Relaxed Flow is properly enabled and aligned with dependencies.
  3. Check for system or resource-related constraints.

If all the above seem correct and the issue persists, you might need to involve your system administrator or functional consultant to investigate further. Let me know if you need help with any specific steps!

This seems to be an issue specific to the SAPUI5 web app in a mobile browser. Could you try opening the web app in mobile browser outside SAP Mobile Start?

I found a similar issue in the past, and you can review the solution provided here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sapui5-error-adding-element-with-duplicate-id/qaq-p/754323.

entry.title: Re: Step-by-Step Guide to Use an Attribute Other Than LoginName for Seamless Authentication
entry.content:
Dear @Vasavi_Rongali ,

Thank you very much  for this blog.

I have the following problem: I provision users from Azure to IAS and then from IAS to SAC.

In Azure, the User Principal Name (UPN) is defined as the email address. When these users are provisioned from Azure to IAS, the UPN from Azure (email address) is mapped as the login name in IAS. This causes an error when the users are provisioned from IAS to SAC using SCIM2:  Request is unparsablle syntacticaly incorrect or violates schema

The target system is SAC (default transformation for SCIM API version 2), and I am using the default transformation code from the IAS SAP Help Portal. However, if the login name field in IAS is not set as an email address, the user provisioning to SAC works without any issues.

How can I resolve this error?

Thank you for any tips.

Best Regards

entry.title: Download Introscope Agent 10.7 SP0 Patch 1
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

Can you please let us know exact patch from marketplace where we can download above patch ( Introscope Agent 10.7 SP0 Patch 1 ) . As we could not find the same.

 

Please confirm.

 

Regards,

Basis Team


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Download Introscope Agent 10.7 SP0 Patch 1
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Thanks for the response.

For 10.7 SP0 Patch 1 (ISAGENTJ500P_1.SCA,ISAGNTJ5STD00P_1.ZIP, build 10.7.0.279) 2022-12-14, the patch file from SAP Market Place, it can be found at:

support.sap.com -> Download Software -> Support Packages and Patches -> By Alphabetical Index (A-Z) -> 'S' -> SAP SOLUTION MANAGER -> SAP SOLUTION MANAGER 7.2 -> Solution Manager JAVA Stack ->  
WILY INTRO AGT10.7 MIN J5VIASM (NO LONGER IN MAINTENANCE) ->  ISAGENTJ500P_1-70005239.SCA

The update method on the SAP Solution Manager is deploy the file to the SAP Solution Manager JAVA stack using the Software Update Manager (SUM)-> SAP Note 1641062 - Single component update and patch scenarios in SUM.

The update method for ISAgent on the Managed System:

  1. Execute the activity 'Byte Code Adapter Installation' of the step 'Configure Automatically' in the 'Managed System Configuration' for the affected systems. This activity will deploy the new version of the Introscope JAVA agent in the managed system servers and reconfigure the JVM parameters to use the new version of Introscope JAVA Agent;

  2. Restart the Java stack of the Managed System, for which the activity 'Byte Code Adapter Installation' in the above step has been executed

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Mandatory business volume selection criteria in Condition Contract
entry.content:
Hi all,

We experienced a case where the user created a Condition Contract for an IC customer. In the business Volume selection criteria the user only entered customer number and distribution channel.

This IC customer is used across several sales organizations belonging to different company codes. So, what happened is that accruals was created for 2 company codes for this contract. This in turn caused troubles when we tried to reverse the accruals as we have not allowed the settlement program to post to different company codes (Badi not active). We solved this by simulating that the badi was active in the debugger and then reverse the accruals.

But my question is: Is there any way to make a specific business volume selection criteria mandatory entry? I want to make sales organization ad a mandatory business volume selection criteria.

Br,

Petter

entry.title: Storing 10 Years of Compensation Statements in Employee Profile
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I have a customer requirement to store up to 10 years of Compensation Statements in the Employee Profile. The goal is to ensure these documents remain accessible for a decade, linked directly within each employee's profile.

I would like to know if anyone is aware of any limitations or best practices related to this. Specifically:

  1. Are there any system limitations in terms of storing and retrieving up to 10 compensation statements per employee over this period?
  2. Could having up to 10 compensation statements in the profile lead to performance issues, especially in terms of loading times or system responsiveness?
  3. Are there any recommendations on how to efficiently manage and store such a large amount of compensation data without impacting system performance?

Any insights or experiences on this would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: Re: F-04/FB05- Need to write BDC in ECC
entry.content:
You can use this code to solve your problem. This is bvasically for FB05 tcode 'Transfer Posting and Cleaning' (4th one radiobutton).

NOTE : while uploading the data from excel file, we have to make one refrence field on excel. in my case i create one refrence field that is so_no.  That so SO_NO should be unik for each Account No (RF05A-NEWKO).

There is no use of this SO_NO on screen. but using this refrence field we can upload the excel data. so please don't forget to maintain your excel file with additional col i.e. SO_NO.

if your account no is same for multiple DEBIT and CREDIT. so, against of all this account no SO_NO should be same on excel file. below you can find how to maintain excel file  FOR EXAMPLE, 

SO_NO     BLDAT            BUDAT             BLART       BUKRS ......... NEWBS(D/C)    NEWKO(A/C NO)  AMOUNT

1              20.12.2024     20.12.2024       SA              XYZ                40                     123456                100.00

1              20.12.2024     20.12.2024       SA              XYZ                40                     123456                100.00

1              20.12.2024     20.12.2024       SA              XYZ                50                     123456                200.00

2              20.12.2024     20.12.2024       SA              XYZ                40                     567890                500.00

2              20.12.2024     20.12.2024       SA              XYZ                40                     567890                200.00

2              20.12.2024     20.12.2024       SA              XYZ                50                     567890                700.00  

"Below code you can use for your reference.

report ZFB055
       no standard page heading line-size 255.

**include bdcrecx1.
**
**start-of-selection.

**perform open_group.

  TYPES : BEGIN OF ty_upload,
          SO_NO TYPE SYST_TABIX,
          BLDAT TYPE BKPF-BLDAT,  " DOC DATE
          BUDAT TYPE BKPF-BUDAT,  " POS DATE
          BLART TYPE BKPF-BLART,  " DOC TYPE  " SA
          BUKRS TYPE BKPF-BUKRS,  " COMP CODE

*          MONAT TYPE BKPF-MONAT,  " PERIOD    " 9
          WAERS TYPE BKPF-WAERS,  " CURRENCY
          XBLNR TYPE BKPF-XBLNR,  " REFERENCE
          BKTXT TYPE BKPF-BKTXT,  " DOC HED TEXT
          NEWBS TYPE RF05A-NEWBS,  " PST KEY
          NEWKO TYPE RF05A-NEWKO,  " ACCOUNT
          WRBTR TYPE BSEG-WRBTR,   " AMOUNT
          MWSKZ TYPE BSEG-MWSKZ,   " TAX CODE
          BUPLA TYPE BSEG-BUPLA,   " Business Place

**          SGTXT TYPE BSEG-SGTXT,

          GSBER TYPE COBL-GSBER,   " BUSS AREA
          KOSTL TYPE COBL-KOSTL,   " COST CENTER
          AUFNR TYPE COBL-AUFNR,   " INT ORD NO
          PRCTR TYPE COBL-PRCTR,   " PROFIT CENTER
*          segment TYPE cobl-SEGMENT, " segment
          ZUONR TYPE BSEG-ZUONR,   " Assignment Number
**          SEGMENT TYPE COBL-SEGMENT,
          END OF   ty_upload.


  DATA :  IT_UPLOAD TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF TY_UPLOAD,
          WA_UPLOAD TYPE TY_UPLOAD,
          it_upload1 TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_upload,
          wa_upload1 TYPE ty_upload,
          IT_UPLOAD2 TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF TY_UPLOAD,
          WA_UPLOAD2 TYPE TY_UPLOAD.


  DATA:   IT_TEMP TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF TY_UPLOAD,
          WA_TEMP TYPE TY_UPLOAD.

  DATA:  IT_RAW    TYPE TRUXS_T_TEXT_DATA.

  DATA:  CTU_PARAMS TYPE CTU_PARAMS.

  DATA:  IT_BDCDATA TYPE TABLE OF BDCDATA,
         WA_BDCDATA TYPE BDCDATA,
         IT_MSG TYPE TABLE OF BDCMSGCOLL,
         WA_MSG TYPE BDCMSGCOLL.

       DATA : DATE(10) TYPE C,
              DATE1(10) TYPE C.

*       w_var(10) type c.


  DATA:lv_count       TYPE c,
     lv_text(255)   TYPE c,
     lv_text1(255)  TYPE c,
     lv_text2(255)  TYPE c,
     lv_text3(255)  TYPE c,
     lv_text4(255)  TYPE c,
     lv_text5(255)  TYPE c,
     lv_text6(255)  TYPE c,
     lv_text7(255)  TYPE c,
     l_message      TYPE string,
*     lv_menge(13)   TYPE c.
     lv_menge   TYPE bdc_fval.

*DATA:it_raw         TYPE truxs_t_text_data.
Data: lv_flag type c,
      Lv_index(4) type c value '0000'.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& SELECTION-SCREEN
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
PARAMETERS: p_file TYPE rlgrap-filename ,
            MODE_B RADIOBUTTON GROUP G1 DEFAULT 'X',
            MODE_F RADIOBUTTON GROUP G1.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& AT SELECTION-SCREEN
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_file .

  CALL FUNCTION 'F4_FILENAME'
    EXPORTING
      program_name  = syst-cprog
      dynpro_number = syst-dynnr
      field_name    = 'P_FILE'
    IMPORTING
      file_name     = p_file.

**INITIALIZATION.
**  lv_count = '00'.


START-OF-SELECTION.
  CALL FUNCTION 'TEXT_CONVERT_XLS_TO_SAP'
     EXPORTING
      i_field_seperator           = '#'
      i_line_header               = 'X'
      i_tab_raw_data             = it_raw
      i_filename                 = p_file
     TABLES
       i_tab_converted_data       = it_upload
   EXCEPTIONS
     CONVERSION_FAILED          = 1
     OTHERS                     = 2
             .
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  ENDIF.


SORT IT_UPLOAD by so_no.
*SORT IT_UPLOAD by NEWBS.

IT_UPLOAD1 = IT_UPLOAD.
DELETE ADJACENT DUPLICATES FROM IT_UPLOAD COMPARING SO_NO.

LOOP AT IT_UPLOAD INTO WA_UPLOAD. " from sy-TABIX.
  CONCATENATE WA_UPLOAD-BLDAT+6(2) WA_UPLOAD-BLDAT+4(2) WA_UPLOAD-BLDAT+0(4) INTO DATE SEPARATED BY '.'.
  CONCATENATE WA_UPLOAD-BUDAT+6(2) WA_UPLOAD-BUDAT+4(2) WA_UPLOAD-BUDAT+0(4) INTO DATE1 SEPARATED BY '.'.

perform bdc_dynpro      using 'SAPMF05A' '0122'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_CURSOR'
                              'RF05A-NEWKO'. "NEWKO  XPOS1
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '/00'.

perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-BLDAT'   DATE. "WA_UPLOAD-BLDAT.
*                              '30.11.2024'.

perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-BLART'   WA_UPLOAD-BLART.
*                              'SA'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-BUKRS'   WA_UPLOAD-BUKRS.
*                              'cpil'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-BUDAT'   DATE1. "WA_UPLOAD-BUDAT.
*                              '30.11.2024'.
**perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-MONAT'   WA_UPLOAD-MONAT.
*                              '9'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-WAERS'   WA_UPLOAD-WAERS.
*                              'inr'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-XBLNR'   WA_UPLOAD-XBLNR.
*                              'Test'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-BKTXT'   WA_UPLOAD-BKTXT.
*                              'Test'.
perform bdc_field       using 'FS006-DOCID'
                              '*'.
perform bdc_field       using 'RF05A-NEWBS'  WA_UPLOAD-NEWBS.
*                              '40'.
perform bdc_field       using 'RF05A-NEWKO'  WA_UPLOAD-NEWKO.
*                              '40400650'.


REFRESH IT_UPLOAD2.
LOOP AT IT_UPLOAD1 INTO WA_UPLOAD1 WHERE SO_NO = WA_UPLOAD-SO_NO.
  APPEND WA_UPLOAD1 TO IT_UPLOAD2.
  CLEAR : WA_UPLOAD1.
ENDLOOP.

CLEAR : SY-TABIX.

LOOP AT IT_UPLOAD2 INTO WA_UPLOAD2.
  LV_COUNT = SY-TABIX.

perform bdc_dynpro      using 'SAPMF05A' '0300'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_CURSOR'   "WA_UPLOAD-NEWKO.
                              'RF05A-NEWKO'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '/00'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-WRBTR'   WA_UPLOAD2-WRBTR.
*                              '1000'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-MWSKZ'   WA_UPLOAD2-MWSKZ.
*                              'V0'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-BUPLA'   WA_UPLOAD2-BUPLA.
*                              'HO01'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-ZUONR'   WA_UPLOAD2-ZUONR.
**                              '123456'.
**perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-SGTXT'
**                              'Test'.



LV_COUNT = LV_COUNT + 1.
READ TABLE IT_UPLOAD2 ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<FS_TEMP>) INDEX LV_COUNT.

IF SY-SUBRC = 0 AND <FS_TEMP>-SO_NO EQ WA_UPLOAD2-SO_NO.


perform bdc_field       using 'RF05A-NEWBS'  <FS_TEMP>-NEWBS.
*                              '50'.
perform bdc_field       using 'RF05A-NEWKO'  <FS_TEMP>-NEWKO.
*                              '40400650'.

perform bdc_dynpro      using 'SAPLKACB' '0002'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_CURSOR'   "WA_UPLOAD-PRCTR.
                              'COBL-PRCTR'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '=ENTE'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-GSBER'   WA_UPLOAD2-GSBER.
*                              'ho01'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-KOSTL'   WA_UPLOAD2-KOSTL.
*                              '50078867'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-AUFNR'   WA_UPLOAD2-AUFNR.
*                              '000002113431'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-PRCTR'   WA_UPLOAD2-PRCTR.
*                              '5001cp05'.

ELSE.

perform bdc_dynpro      using 'SAPLKACB' '0002'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_CURSOR'    "WA_UPLOAD-PRCTR.
                              'COBL-PRCTR'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '=ENTE'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-GSBER'   WA_UPLOAD2-GSBER.
*                              'ho01'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-KOSTL'   WA_UPLOAD2-KOSTL.
*                              '50010501'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-AUFNR'   WA_UPLOAD2-AUFNR.
*                              '000002113431'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-PRCTR'   WA_UPLOAD2-PRCTR.
*                              '5001cp05'.


perform bdc_dynpro      using 'SAPMF05A' '0300'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_CURSOR'   "WA_UPLOAD-WRBTR.
                              'BSEG-WRBTR'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '=BU'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-WRBTR'   WA_UPLOAD2-WRBTR.
*                              '1,000.00'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-MWSKZ'   WA_UPLOAD2-MWSKZ.
*                              'V0'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-BUPLA'   WA_UPLOAD2-BUPLA.
**                              'HO01'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-ZUONR'   WA_UPLOAD2-ZUONR.
**                              '123456'.
**perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-SGTXT'
**                              'Test'.
perform bdc_field       using 'DKACB-FMORE'
                              'X'.

perform bdc_dynpro      using 'SAPLKACB' '0002'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_CURSOR'   "WA_UPLOAD-GSBER.
                              'COBL-GSBER'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '=ENTE'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-GSBER'   WA_UPLOAD2-GSBER.
*                              'HO01'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-KOSTL'   WA_UPLOAD2-KOSTL.
*                              '50010501'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-AUFNR'   WA_UPLOAD2-AUFNR.
*                              '2113431'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-PRCTR'   WA_UPLOAD2-PRCTR.
*                              '5001CP05'.
*perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-SEGMENT' "WA_UPLOAD-SEGMENT.
*                              'IRON.
EXIT.
ENDIF.

CLEAR : WA_UPLOAD2.
ENDLOOP.

IF MODE_B = 'X'.
    CALL TRANSACTION 'FB05' USING IT_BDCDATA MODE 'N' UPDATE 'A' MESSAGES INTO IT_MSG.
ENDIF.

IF MODE_F = 'X'.
    CALL TRANSACTION 'FB05' USING IT_BDCDATA MODE 'A' UPDATE 'A' MESSAGES INTO IT_MSG.
ENDIF.

    REFRESH IT_BDCDATA.
    CLEAR : WA_UPLOAD.
ENDLOOP.

LOOP AT it_msg INTO wa_msg .

    CALL FUNCTION 'FORMAT_MESSAGE'
      EXPORTING
        id        = wa_msg-msgid
        lang      = sy-langu
        no        = wa_msg-msgnr
        v1        = wa_msg-msgv1
        v2        = wa_msg-msgv2
        v3        = wa_msg-msgv3
        v4        = wa_msg-msgv4
      IMPORTING
        msg       = l_message
      EXCEPTIONS
        not_found = 1
        OTHERS    = 2.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
*         WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
    ENDIF.

    WRITE:/ l_message .
  ENDLOOP .
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*        Start new screen                                              *
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM bdc_dynpro USING program dynpro.
  CLEAR wa_bdcdata.
  wa_bdcdata-program  = program.
  wa_bdcdata-dynpro   = dynpro.
  wa_bdcdata-dynbegin = 'X'.
  APPEND wa_bdcdata TO it_bdcdata.
ENDFORM.                    "

*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*        Insert field                                                  *
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM bdc_field USING fnam fval.
*  IF FVAL <> NODATA.
  CLEAR wa_bdcdata.
  wa_bdcdata-fnam = fnam.
  wa_bdcdata-fval = fval.
  SHIFT wa_bdcdata-fval LEFT DELETING LEADING SPACE.
  APPEND wa_bdcdata TO it_bdcdata.
*  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.

Best Regard,

Rishabh.

entry.title: Re: Test Tenant does not Exist in Export Customizing Tran...
entry.content:
Hello Ariel,

Are you on 3SL or 2SL? If it is a new project, i assume you have 3SL (3-system landscape).

If so, the tenants (clients) are:

  • DEV: 100 for Customizing, 80 for Abap steampunk development
  • TEST: 100
  • PROD: 100

You always have just 1 CBC instance, this is linked primarily to the DEV 100 tenant.

The deployment target from CBC is the DEV 100 tenant (customizing tenant). CBC will send high level configuration there (like primary financial settings, scope items, org structure).

When this step is done, all further customizing can be done directly on DEV 100 (App "Implementation Activities" you do not need to go through CBC for most settings)

And the on DEV 100 with App "Export Customizing Transports" you release them and transport them to the TEST 100 tenant. Do not forget to export/import the SW_Collections as well (Extensibility items).

Regards,

Johannes

entry.title: Re: Apps for spool requests in SAP Public cloud
entry.content:
Hello Kazuhiro,

Since public cloud needs Cloud Print Manager for printing in local network, there is the printer queue managed in the cloud and pulled by Cloud print manager. You should see the spool requests in App "Manage Print Queues" in the Cloud, or probably you can login to print manager (locally) and check the queue items there.

Johannes

entry.title: Re: sap analytics cloud data action advanced formula to d...
entry.content:
Hi @hsthind 

If the date dimension granularity is Month, please try the data action script below to get your expected result.

 

MEMBERSET [d/Measures] = "Amount"
MEMBERSET [d/CostCenter] = "CC01"
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = "202401" TO "202412"
MEMBERSET [d/GLAccount] = "GL01"

VARIABLEMEMBER #NO_Week OF [d/GLAccount]
FLOAT @Sum_OF_Value

IF MOD(MONTH([d/Date]), 3) = 0 THEN
	DATA([d/GLAccount] = #NO_Week, [d/CostCenter] = "#") = 5
ELSE
	DATA([d/GLAccount] = #NO_Week, [d/CostCenter] = "#") = 4
ENDIF

@Sum_OF_Value = RESULTLOOKUP()
DATA() = @Sum_OF_Value * RESULTLOOKUP([d/GLAccount] = #NO_Week, [d/CostCenter] = "#") / 52

 

On my testing, I got the following result: 

 

I hope this is helpful to you, and if I have misunderstood anything, please feel free to reach out.

 

Moonjun

entry.title: Workaround solution to catalogs stuck in Activating status within SAP Ariba Catalogs
entry.content:
SAP Ariba Catalogs represents a centralized solution that allows organizations to upload supplier catalogs so the employees are provided with option to search and procure products or services they need. Catalog administrators import catalogs that firstly go through validation process and once there are no issues found with concerned catalog, it's moved afterwards into Activating status that should later move into Activated status which means that catalog is published and items from given catalog are available for end users in Catalog UI.
Although most of the time catalog activation process is smooth and catalogs are getting activated in timespan from few minutes up to few hours for large catalogs with thousands of items, from time to time some catalogs might get stuck in Activating status without getting into Activated status. There might be various causes for this, for example performance issues, issues with catalog index builder etc., many times these issues are only temporary.
When catalog is stuck in Activating status for more than 1 day it also means that without any action, catalog will remain stuck. Before asking SAP Ariba support for help with unstucking and moving the catalog into Activated status, following approach can help to move catalog into Activated status:

1. When catalog is stuck in Activating status, select the catalog (or latest catalog version if catalog has previous versions) using checkbox and click on button "Deactivate"
2. On the next screen confirm the deactivation by clicking again on button "Deactivate", then click on "Catalog Home" button to get back to previous screen
3. Catalog is now moved to Deactivating status (and if catalog has more versions, previous version of catalog is then moved to "Activating" status)
4. Immediately afterwards again select concerned catalog version and now click on button "Activate" for same catalog - there's no need to wait for deactivation process to finish
5. On the next screen confirm the activation by clicking again on button "Activate", then click on "Catalog Home" button to get back to previous screen
6. Catalog is then again moved to Activating status (and if catalog has more versions, previous version of catalog is then moved to “Deactivating” status). Activation process for concerned catalog is now basically re-run again and if catalog was previously stuck in Activating status due to some temporary issue, this second activation run should be successful - so catalog will get moved into Activated status.

Similar approach can be applied also for catalogs stuck in Deactivating status (if intention is to deactivate catalog), only difference is that it's needed to firstly click on "Activate" button and then again on "Deactivate" button so the deactivation process is re-run.
Anyway, if aproach described above does not help and concerned catalog still remains stuck in Activating (or Deactivating) status, then a ticket for SAP Ariba support needs to be submitted for further help.

entry.title: Community feedback and contributions in 2025
entry.content:
As we are all slowly starting to wind down 2024 and at the same time prepare for 2025, I wanted to take the opportunity to share some information about community feedback and how you can contribute to the community next year. 

First of all, I would like to start off by assuring everyone that we have heard your feedback this year, both here and on other non-SAP platforms. Everything has been captured. 

Some of the feedback could be attributed to getting used to working on a new platform and getting accustomed to new tools, so we have diligently updated our FAQ/Help Page to help with the most common "how do I" questions we received after the migration earlier this year. The FAQ will of course continuously be updated also in 2025. 

Other feedback was captured as enhancement requests and bugs, and some of these were added to our 2024 releases. More information about these can be found in our What's New section:

  1. June release
  2. August release
  3. October and November releases

We have heard your request for a clearly defined process for how you can provide feedback, and what happens to that feedback once submitted. We are therefore very happy to announce a more transparent feedback process for 2025.

We will start with launching a brand new ideation board in the community. The ideation board will: 

  • give us a better understanding of what matters most to you as you will be able to upvote submitted ideas. 
  • be used if we need clarifications or more information, as we have a shared public space for communication. 
  • give the Community team an opportunity to provide continuous updates on submitted ideas and feedback, as each entry will change status as we process them. (the different statuses are: New, Needs Info, Investigating, Accepted, Declined, Delivered)

To avoid double entries and repeating received feedback, the first version of the ideation board we release will be pre-populated with feedback we have received in 2024, so that you begin voting on what we already have captured. This way we can get experience working with the tool first, and make sure it works as expected both for us and for you. 

If there is feedback we have missed, or you would like to submit new ideas to the board, please send us an email at community@sap.com or use the Questions about SAP Websites Q&A. We will add the ideas to the board so that other community members can see the feedback and vote. 
(a second phase for the ideation board will be to open it for submitting new ideas as well)

The ideation board is still work in progress, and we do not have a confirmed release date yet.
To start the journey of being more transparent about what we are working on, we felt it was a good place to start here, even if it is not 100% ready yet. We will keep you up to date on progress, and of course also share the go-live date as soon as we have it. 
Please be patient with us while we work as fast as we can to add everyone's feedback to the board, with as many details as possible. 

Thank you everyone for your contributions and for keeping the community spirit alive in 2024. 
We are all excited for 2025, making sure the SAP Community continues to thrive together with you.

The entire SAP Community team wishes everyone happy holidays and we look forward to reconnecting in the new year!

entry.title: Need to change open Item Management and the Tax Category for some G/L account.
entry.content:
We are facing issues with Open Item Management and the Tax Category for some G/L account.

 

For the given G/L accounts in the attachments, Open Item Management is set to False and needs to be changed to True and also for some G/L accounts, the Tax Category needs to be changed.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Need to change open Item Management and the Tax Categ...
entry.content:
1. Open Item Management is set to False and needs to be changed to True.
-->First, it depends on which kind of open item management you use. Different methods are possible for open item management, open item management by ledger group, and extended open item management. For example, for open item management, when you try to save a change to the Open Item Management indicator in the Manage G/L Account Master Data app, the development system communicates with the production system to check if it’s possible to make such a change. The production system checks and makes sure that the affected account has a zero balance and no postings. If the systems determine that the change is allowed, it is included in the next transport from the development system to the production system. If the systems determine the change isn’t allowed, you must schedule a job in the Schedule Accounting Data Changes app using the Activate Open Item Management for G/L Accounts job template. For more information about all three kinds of open item management, see Open Item Management for G/L Accounts.

2. The Tax Category needs to be changed for some g/l account.
--> When you try to save a change to the tax category for a G/L account in the Manage G/L Account Master Data app, the development system checks that the account has a zero balance in that system and the production system. If there are postings in a system, the change is not possible. In this case, create a new G/L account with the correct tax category for future postings or follow these steps:

  1. Create a dummy G/L account in the development system and transport the change to the test and the production system.

  2. Move the balance to this new account temporarily in all the systems of your system landscape.

  3. Change the tax category in the original G/L account in the development system and transport the change to the test and the production system.

  4. Return the balance to the original G/L account in all systems of your system landscape.

  5. Select the G/L Account Not Used indicator for the dummy account in the development system and transport the change to the test and the production system.

Kindly go through the below SAP help portal for your reference:-
G/L Account Master Data: FAQ | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Mandatory business volume selection criteria in Condi...
entry.content:
Hi Petter, 

you can use the setting below (Define and configure Sets of Field Combinations). 

Minimum Number of Entries for Field Combination

Specifies the minimum number of entries for a field combination in the business volume selection for a condition contract.

Use

When you enter a value, it makes the field combination mandatory for the business volume selection.

When you enter a value for a field combination with an assigned field combination group, the system performs the check at field combination group level. This means that the minimum number of entries for all field combinations of the group is totaled and checked against the number of business volume selection criteria entries for the field combinations of the group.

Example

The field combinations FC1 and FC2 have field combination group A. You have entered 1 as the minimum number for FC1 and you have not made an entry for FC2. This means that you have to enter at least one entry for FC1 or FC2 in the business volume selection criteria of a condition contract.

entry.title: Re: F-04/FB05- Need to write BDC
entry.content:
BDC FOR FB05 tcode 

You can use this code to solve your problem. This is basically for FB05 tcode 'Transfer Posting and Cleaning' (4th one radiobutton).

NOTE : while uploading the data from excel file, we have to make one refrence field on excel. in my case i create one refrence field that is so_no.  That so SO_NO should be unik for each Account No (RF05A-NEWKO).

There is no use of this SO_NO on screen. but using this refrence field we can upload the excel data. so please don't forget to maintain your excel file with additional col i.e. SO_NO.

if your account no is same for multiple DEBIT and CREDIT. so, against of all this account no SO_NO should be same on excel file. below you can find how to maintain excel file  FOR EXAMPLE, 

SO_NO     BLDAT            BUDAT             BLART       BUKRS ......... NEWBS(D/C)    NEWKO(A/C NO)  AMOUNT

1              20.12.2024     20.12.2024       SA              XYZ                40                     123456                100.00

1              20.12.2024     20.12.2024       SA              XYZ                40                     123456                100.00

1              20.12.2024     20.12.2024       SA              XYZ                50                     123456                200.00

2              20.12.2024     20.12.2024       SA              XYZ                40                     567890                500.00

2              20.12.2024     20.12.2024       SA              XYZ                40                     567890                200.00

2              20.12.2024     20.12.2024       SA              XYZ                50                     567890                700.00  

"Below code you can use for your reference.

report ZFB055
       no standard page heading line-size 255.

**include bdcrecx1.
**
**start-of-selection.

**perform open_group.

  TYPES : BEGIN OF ty_upload,
          SO_NO TYPE SYST_TABIX,
          BLDAT TYPE BKPF-BLDAT,  " DOC DATE
          BUDAT TYPE BKPF-BUDAT,  " POS DATE
          BLART TYPE BKPF-BLART,  " DOC TYPE  " SA
          BUKRS TYPE BKPF-BUKRS,  " COMP CODE

*          MONAT TYPE BKPF-MONAT,  " PERIOD    " 9
          WAERS TYPE BKPF-WAERS,  " CURRENCY
          XBLNR TYPE BKPF-XBLNR,  " REFERENCE
          BKTXT TYPE BKPF-BKTXT,  " DOC HED TEXT
          NEWBS TYPE RF05A-NEWBS,  " PST KEY
          NEWKO TYPE RF05A-NEWKO,  " ACCOUNT
          WRBTR TYPE BSEG-WRBTR,   " AMOUNT
          MWSKZ TYPE BSEG-MWSKZ,   " TAX CODE
          BUPLA TYPE BSEG-BUPLA,   " Business Place

**          SGTXT TYPE BSEG-SGTXT,

          GSBER TYPE COBL-GSBER,   " BUSS AREA
          KOSTL TYPE COBL-KOSTL,   " COST CENTER
          AUFNR TYPE COBL-AUFNR,   " INT ORD NO
          PRCTR TYPE COBL-PRCTR,   " PROFIT CENTER
*          segment TYPE cobl-SEGMENT, " segment
          ZUONR TYPE BSEG-ZUONR,   " Assignment Number
**          SEGMENT TYPE COBL-SEGMENT,
          END OF   ty_upload.


  DATA :  IT_UPLOAD TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF TY_UPLOAD,
          WA_UPLOAD TYPE TY_UPLOAD,
          it_upload1 TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_upload,
          wa_upload1 TYPE ty_upload,
          IT_UPLOAD2 TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF TY_UPLOAD,
          WA_UPLOAD2 TYPE TY_UPLOAD.


  DATA:   IT_TEMP TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF TY_UPLOAD,
          WA_TEMP TYPE TY_UPLOAD.

  DATA:  IT_RAW    TYPE TRUXS_T_TEXT_DATA.

  DATA:  CTU_PARAMS TYPE CTU_PARAMS.

  DATA:  IT_BDCDATA TYPE TABLE OF BDCDATA,
         WA_BDCDATA TYPE BDCDATA,
         IT_MSG TYPE TABLE OF BDCMSGCOLL,
         WA_MSG TYPE BDCMSGCOLL.

       DATA : DATE(10) TYPE C,
              DATE1(10) TYPE C.

*       w_var(10) type c.


  DATA:lv_count       TYPE c,
     lv_text(255)   TYPE c,
     lv_text1(255)  TYPE c,
     lv_text2(255)  TYPE c,
     lv_text3(255)  TYPE c,
     lv_text4(255)  TYPE c,
     lv_text5(255)  TYPE c,
     lv_text6(255)  TYPE c,
     lv_text7(255)  TYPE c,
     l_message      TYPE string,
*     lv_menge(13)   TYPE c.
     lv_menge   TYPE bdc_fval.

*DATA:it_raw         TYPE truxs_t_text_data.
Data: lv_flag type c,
      Lv_index(4) type c value '0000'.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& SELECTION-SCREEN
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
PARAMETERS: p_file TYPE rlgrap-filename ,
            MODE_B RADIOBUTTON GROUP G1 DEFAULT 'X',
            MODE_F RADIOBUTTON GROUP G1.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& AT SELECTION-SCREEN
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_file .

  CALL FUNCTION 'F4_FILENAME'
    EXPORTING
      program_name  = syst-cprog
      dynpro_number = syst-dynnr
      field_name    = 'P_FILE'
    IMPORTING
      file_name     = p_file.

**INITIALIZATION.
**  lv_count = '00'.


START-OF-SELECTION.
  CALL FUNCTION 'TEXT_CONVERT_XLS_TO_SAP'
     EXPORTING
      i_field_seperator           = '#'
      i_line_header               = 'X'
      i_tab_raw_data             = it_raw
      i_filename                 = p_file
     TABLES
       i_tab_converted_data       = it_upload
   EXCEPTIONS
     CONVERSION_FAILED          = 1
     OTHERS                     = 2
             .
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  ENDIF.


SORT IT_UPLOAD by so_no.
*SORT IT_UPLOAD by NEWBS.

IT_UPLOAD1 = IT_UPLOAD.
DELETE ADJACENT DUPLICATES FROM IT_UPLOAD COMPARING SO_NO.

LOOP AT IT_UPLOAD INTO WA_UPLOAD. " from sy-TABIX.
  CONCATENATE WA_UPLOAD-BLDAT+6(2) WA_UPLOAD-BLDAT+4(2) WA_UPLOAD-BLDAT+0(4) INTO DATE SEPARATED BY '.'.
  CONCATENATE WA_UPLOAD-BUDAT+6(2) WA_UPLOAD-BUDAT+4(2) WA_UPLOAD-BUDAT+0(4) INTO DATE1 SEPARATED BY '.'.

perform bdc_dynpro      using 'SAPMF05A' '0122'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_CURSOR'
                              'RF05A-NEWKO'. "NEWKO  XPOS1
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '/00'.

perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-BLDAT'   DATE. "WA_UPLOAD-BLDAT.
*                              '30.11.2024'.

perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-BLART'   WA_UPLOAD-BLART.
*                              'SA'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-BUKRS'   WA_UPLOAD-BUKRS.
*                              'cpil'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-BUDAT'   DATE1. "WA_UPLOAD-BUDAT.
*                              '30.11.2024'.
**perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-MONAT'   WA_UPLOAD-MONAT.
*                              '9'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-WAERS'   WA_UPLOAD-WAERS.
*                              'inr'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-XBLNR'   WA_UPLOAD-XBLNR.
*                              'Test'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-BKTXT'   WA_UPLOAD-BKTXT.
*                              'Test'.
perform bdc_field       using 'FS006-DOCID'
                              '*'.
perform bdc_field       using 'RF05A-NEWBS'  WA_UPLOAD-NEWBS.
*                              '40'.
perform bdc_field       using 'RF05A-NEWKO'  WA_UPLOAD-NEWKO.
*                              '40400650'.


REFRESH IT_UPLOAD2.
LOOP AT IT_UPLOAD1 INTO WA_UPLOAD1 WHERE SO_NO = WA_UPLOAD-SO_NO.
  APPEND WA_UPLOAD1 TO IT_UPLOAD2.
  CLEAR : WA_UPLOAD1.
ENDLOOP.

CLEAR : SY-TABIX.

LOOP AT IT_UPLOAD2 INTO WA_UPLOAD2.
  LV_COUNT = SY-TABIX.

perform bdc_dynpro      using 'SAPMF05A' '0300'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_CURSOR'   "WA_UPLOAD-NEWKO.
                              'RF05A-NEWKO'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '/00'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-WRBTR'   WA_UPLOAD2-WRBTR.
*                              '1000'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-MWSKZ'   WA_UPLOAD2-MWSKZ.
*                              'V0'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-BUPLA'   WA_UPLOAD2-BUPLA.
*                              'HO01'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-ZUONR'   WA_UPLOAD2-ZUONR.
**                              '123456'.
**perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-SGTXT'
**                              'Test'.



LV_COUNT = LV_COUNT + 1.
READ TABLE IT_UPLOAD2 ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<FS_TEMP>) INDEX LV_COUNT.

IF SY-SUBRC = 0 AND <FS_TEMP>-SO_NO EQ WA_UPLOAD2-SO_NO.


perform bdc_field       using 'RF05A-NEWBS'  <FS_TEMP>-NEWBS.
*                              '50'.
perform bdc_field       using 'RF05A-NEWKO'  <FS_TEMP>-NEWKO.
*                              '40400650'.

perform bdc_dynpro      using 'SAPLKACB' '0002'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_CURSOR'   "WA_UPLOAD-PRCTR.
                              'COBL-PRCTR'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '=ENTE'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-GSBER'   WA_UPLOAD2-GSBER.
*                              'ho01'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-KOSTL'   WA_UPLOAD2-KOSTL.
*                              '50078867'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-AUFNR'   WA_UPLOAD2-AUFNR.
*                              '000002113431'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-PRCTR'   WA_UPLOAD2-PRCTR.
*                              '5001cp05'.

ELSE.

perform bdc_dynpro      using 'SAPLKACB' '0002'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_CURSOR'    "WA_UPLOAD-PRCTR.
                              'COBL-PRCTR'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '=ENTE'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-GSBER'   WA_UPLOAD2-GSBER.
*                              'ho01'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-KOSTL'   WA_UPLOAD2-KOSTL.
*                              '50010501'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-AUFNR'   WA_UPLOAD2-AUFNR.
*                              '000002113431'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-PRCTR'   WA_UPLOAD2-PRCTR.
*                              '5001cp05'.


perform bdc_dynpro      using 'SAPMF05A' '0300'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_CURSOR'   "WA_UPLOAD-WRBTR.
                              'BSEG-WRBTR'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '=BU'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-WRBTR'   WA_UPLOAD2-WRBTR.
*                              '1,000.00'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-MWSKZ'   WA_UPLOAD2-MWSKZ.
*                              'V0'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-BUPLA'   WA_UPLOAD2-BUPLA.
**                              'HO01'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-ZUONR'   WA_UPLOAD2-ZUONR.
**                              '123456'.
**perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-SGTXT'
**                              'Test'.
perform bdc_field       using 'DKACB-FMORE'
                              'X'.

perform bdc_dynpro      using 'SAPLKACB' '0002'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_CURSOR'   "WA_UPLOAD-GSBER.
                              'COBL-GSBER'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '=ENTE'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-GSBER'   WA_UPLOAD2-GSBER.
*                              'HO01'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-KOSTL'   WA_UPLOAD2-KOSTL.
*                              '50010501'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-AUFNR'   WA_UPLOAD2-AUFNR.
*                              '2113431'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-PRCTR'   WA_UPLOAD2-PRCTR.
*                              '5001CP05'.
*perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-SEGMENT' "WA_UPLOAD-SEGMENT.
*                              'IRON.
EXIT.
ENDIF.

CLEAR : WA_UPLOAD2.
ENDLOOP.

IF MODE_B = 'X'.
    CALL TRANSACTION 'FB05' USING IT_BDCDATA MODE 'N' UPDATE 'A' MESSAGES INTO IT_MSG.
ENDIF.

IF MODE_F = 'X'.
    CALL TRANSACTION 'FB05' USING IT_BDCDATA MODE 'A' UPDATE 'A' MESSAGES INTO IT_MSG.
ENDIF.

    REFRESH IT_BDCDATA.
    CLEAR : WA_UPLOAD.
ENDLOOP.

LOOP AT it_msg INTO wa_msg .

    CALL FUNCTION 'FORMAT_MESSAGE'
      EXPORTING
        id        = wa_msg-msgid
        lang      = sy-langu
        no        = wa_msg-msgnr
        v1        = wa_msg-msgv1
        v2        = wa_msg-msgv2
        v3        = wa_msg-msgv3
        v4        = wa_msg-msgv4
      IMPORTING
        msg       = l_message
      EXCEPTIONS
        not_found = 1
        OTHERS    = 2.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
*         WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
    ENDIF.

    WRITE:/ l_message .
  ENDLOOP .
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*        Start new screen                                              *
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM bdc_dynpro USING program dynpro.
  CLEAR wa_bdcdata.
  wa_bdcdata-program  = program.
  wa_bdcdata-dynpro   = dynpro.
  wa_bdcdata-dynbegin = 'X'.
  APPEND wa_bdcdata TO it_bdcdata.
ENDFORM.                    "

*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*        Insert field                                                  *
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM bdc_field USING fnam fval.
*  IF FVAL <> NODATA.
  CLEAR wa_bdcdata.
  wa_bdcdata-fnam = fnam.
  wa_bdcdata-fval = fval.
  SHIFT wa_bdcdata-fval LEFT DELETING LEADING SPACE.
  APPEND wa_bdcdata TO it_bdcdata.
*  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.

Best Regard,

Rishabh.

dont't forget to give me rewards 😃

 

Thank You.

entry.title: Load BW On Hana DSO/CUBE into DataSphere( Configuration)
entry.content:
Hi All,

We got Licensees for Datasphere Premium Outbound and we are trying to connect to BW on Hana system and load DSO/Cubes via Datasphere.

Initially i created an SAP BW connection and was able to successfully connect to our BW system.But unable to view any DSO/Cubes .

Do i need to do any configuration in BW for BW objects to show up in Data sphere.

As of now i could see only my BPC objects.

Once this is successful i will be creating an Abap connection and want to use D.S replication flows to move data to 3rd party systems.

Any help on this is much appreciated.

Thanks,

Chaitanya.

entry.title: About connection settings between SAC and S/4HANA Cloud(1YB)
entry.content:
So far, I have been working on importing and connecting SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Analytics Cloud.

I proceeded with the following steps
Setting Up Import Connection Setup with SAP Analytics Cloud (1YB)
https://support.sap.com/content/dam/SAAP/Sol_Pack/S4C/Library/Setup/1YB_Set-Up_EN_XX.pdf

I created a Communication System and Communication Arrangement on the SAP S/4HANA Cloud side, but I was unable to create a Connection on the SAC side.

If you know of anything to be careful of or a better procedure, please let me know.
Thank you for your comment.

entry.title: Insufficient Resources Error while deploying in CF (BTP Trial)
entry.content:
Good morning,

My team and I have been working with the trial account for some time, developing applications and running tests without any issues. However, starting today, whenever we try to start the applications deployed in CF, they become stuck and never fully initialize.
When attempting to deploy them using mbt build > cf deploy, we encounter the following error:

 

Error staging application "apptests-db-deployer": InsufficientResources - Insufficient resources

 

We’ve tried adjusting the memory and disk parameters to the minimum possible values, but the error persists.
We also tested this on different trial accounts, and the same issue occurs in all of them.

Does anyone have any idea what might be happening? Is this a known, general issue that will be resolved soon?

Please note that we’re working with an Amazon Web Services (AWS) account in the US East (VA) region.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Upgrading to CAP CDS 8: A living document of fixes and lessons learned
entry.content:
Even with "@cds.validate: false" nested associations are not updated on PATCH request with the new odata adapter.
Works fine with the old one.
This is a major issue.

entry.title: Re: F-04/FB05- Need to write BDC
entry.content:
BDC FOR FB05 tcode 

Follow step by step :-

You can use this code to solve your problem. This is bvasically for FB05 tcode 'Transfer Posting and Cleaning' (4th one radiobutton).

NOTE : while uploading the data from excel file, we have to make one refrence field on excel. in my case i create one refrence field that is so_no.  That so SO_NO should be unik for each Account No (RF05A-NEWKO).

There is no use of this SO_NO on screen. but using this refrence field we can upload the excel data. so please don't forget to maintain your excel file with additional col i.e. SO_NO.

if your account no is same for multiple DEBIT and CREDIT. so, against of all this account no SO_NO should be same on excel file. below you can find how to maintain excel file  FOR EXAMPLE, 

SO_NO     BLDAT            BUDAT             BLART       BUKRS ......... NEWBS(D/C)    NEWKO(A/C NO)  AMOUNT

1              20.12.2024     20.12.2024       SA              XYZ                40                     123456                100.00

1              20.12.2024     20.12.2024       SA              XYZ                40                     123456                100.00

1              20.12.2024     20.12.2024       SA              XYZ                50                     123456                200.00

2              20.12.2024     20.12.2024       SA              XYZ                40                     567890                500.00

2              20.12.2024     20.12.2024       SA              XYZ                40                     567890                200.00

2              20.12.2024     20.12.2024       SA              XYZ                50                     567890                700.00  

"Below code you can use for your reference.

report ZFB055
       no standard page heading line-size 255.

**include bdcrecx1.
**
**start-of-selection.

**perform open_group.

  TYPES : BEGIN OF ty_upload,
          SO_NO TYPE SYST_TABIX,
          BLDAT TYPE BKPF-BLDAT,  " DOC DATE
          BUDAT TYPE BKPF-BUDAT,  " POS DATE
          BLART TYPE BKPF-BLART,  " DOC TYPE  " SA
          BUKRS TYPE BKPF-BUKRS,  " COMP CODE

*          MONAT TYPE BKPF-MONAT,  " PERIOD    " 9
          WAERS TYPE BKPF-WAERS,  " CURRENCY
          XBLNR TYPE BKPF-XBLNR,  " REFERENCE
          BKTXT TYPE BKPF-BKTXT,  " DOC HED TEXT
          NEWBS TYPE RF05A-NEWBS,  " PST KEY
          NEWKO TYPE RF05A-NEWKO,  " ACCOUNT
          WRBTR TYPE BSEG-WRBTR,   " AMOUNT
          MWSKZ TYPE BSEG-MWSKZ,   " TAX CODE
          BUPLA TYPE BSEG-BUPLA,   " Business Place

**          SGTXT TYPE BSEG-SGTXT,

          GSBER TYPE COBL-GSBER,   " BUSS AREA
          KOSTL TYPE COBL-KOSTL,   " COST CENTER
          AUFNR TYPE COBL-AUFNR,   " INT ORD NO
          PRCTR TYPE COBL-PRCTR,   " PROFIT CENTER
*          segment TYPE cobl-SEGMENT, " segment
          ZUONR TYPE BSEG-ZUONR,   " Assignment Number
**          SEGMENT TYPE COBL-SEGMENT,
          END OF   ty_upload.


  DATA :  IT_UPLOAD TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF TY_UPLOAD,
          WA_UPLOAD TYPE TY_UPLOAD,
          it_upload1 TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_upload,
          wa_upload1 TYPE ty_upload,
          IT_UPLOAD2 TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF TY_UPLOAD,
          WA_UPLOAD2 TYPE TY_UPLOAD.


  DATA:   IT_TEMP TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF TY_UPLOAD,
          WA_TEMP TYPE TY_UPLOAD.

  DATA:  IT_RAW    TYPE TRUXS_T_TEXT_DATA.

  DATA:  CTU_PARAMS TYPE CTU_PARAMS.

  DATA:  IT_BDCDATA TYPE TABLE OF BDCDATA,
         WA_BDCDATA TYPE BDCDATA,
         IT_MSG TYPE TABLE OF BDCMSGCOLL,
         WA_MSG TYPE BDCMSGCOLL.

       DATA : DATE(10) TYPE C,
              DATE1(10) TYPE C.

*       w_var(10) type c.


  DATA:lv_count       TYPE c,
     lv_text(255)   TYPE c,
     lv_text1(255)  TYPE c,
     lv_text2(255)  TYPE c,
     lv_text3(255)  TYPE c,
     lv_text4(255)  TYPE c,
     lv_text5(255)  TYPE c,
     lv_text6(255)  TYPE c,
     lv_text7(255)  TYPE c,
     l_message      TYPE string,
*     lv_menge(13)   TYPE c.
     lv_menge   TYPE bdc_fval.

*DATA:it_raw         TYPE truxs_t_text_data.
Data: lv_flag type c,
      Lv_index(4) type c value '0000'.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& SELECTION-SCREEN
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
PARAMETERS: p_file TYPE rlgrap-filename ,
            MODE_B RADIOBUTTON GROUP G1 DEFAULT 'X',
            MODE_F RADIOBUTTON GROUP G1.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& AT SELECTION-SCREEN
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_file .

  CALL FUNCTION 'F4_FILENAME'
    EXPORTING
      program_name  = syst-cprog
      dynpro_number = syst-dynnr
      field_name    = 'P_FILE'
    IMPORTING
      file_name     = p_file.

**INITIALIZATION.
**  lv_count = '00'.


START-OF-SELECTION.
  CALL FUNCTION 'TEXT_CONVERT_XLS_TO_SAP'
     EXPORTING
      i_field_seperator           = '#'
      i_line_header               = 'X'
      i_tab_raw_data             = it_raw
      i_filename                 = p_file
     TABLES
       i_tab_converted_data       = it_upload
   EXCEPTIONS
     CONVERSION_FAILED          = 1
     OTHERS                     = 2
             .
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  ENDIF.


SORT IT_UPLOAD by so_no.
*SORT IT_UPLOAD by NEWBS.

IT_UPLOAD1 = IT_UPLOAD.
DELETE ADJACENT DUPLICATES FROM IT_UPLOAD COMPARING SO_NO.

LOOP AT IT_UPLOAD INTO WA_UPLOAD. " from sy-TABIX.
  CONCATENATE WA_UPLOAD-BLDAT+6(2) WA_UPLOAD-BLDAT+4(2) WA_UPLOAD-BLDAT+0(4) INTO DATE SEPARATED BY '.'.
  CONCATENATE WA_UPLOAD-BUDAT+6(2) WA_UPLOAD-BUDAT+4(2) WA_UPLOAD-BUDAT+0(4) INTO DATE1 SEPARATED BY '.'.

perform bdc_dynpro      using 'SAPMF05A' '0122'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_CURSOR'
                              'RF05A-NEWKO'. "NEWKO  XPOS1
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '/00'.

perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-BLDAT'   DATE. "WA_UPLOAD-BLDAT.
*                              '30.11.2024'.

perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-BLART'   WA_UPLOAD-BLART.
*                              'SA'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-BUKRS'   WA_UPLOAD-BUKRS.
*                              'cpil'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-BUDAT'   DATE1. "WA_UPLOAD-BUDAT.
*                              '30.11.2024'.
**perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-MONAT'   WA_UPLOAD-MONAT.
*                              '9'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-WAERS'   WA_UPLOAD-WAERS.
*                              'inr'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-XBLNR'   WA_UPLOAD-XBLNR.
*                              'Test'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BKPF-BKTXT'   WA_UPLOAD-BKTXT.
*                              'Test'.
perform bdc_field       using 'FS006-DOCID'
                              '*'.
perform bdc_field       using 'RF05A-NEWBS'  WA_UPLOAD-NEWBS.
*                              '40'.
perform bdc_field       using 'RF05A-NEWKO'  WA_UPLOAD-NEWKO.
*                              '40400650'.


REFRESH IT_UPLOAD2.
LOOP AT IT_UPLOAD1 INTO WA_UPLOAD1 WHERE SO_NO = WA_UPLOAD-SO_NO.
  APPEND WA_UPLOAD1 TO IT_UPLOAD2.
  CLEAR : WA_UPLOAD1.
ENDLOOP.

CLEAR : SY-TABIX.

LOOP AT IT_UPLOAD2 INTO WA_UPLOAD2.
  LV_COUNT = SY-TABIX.

perform bdc_dynpro      using 'SAPMF05A' '0300'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_CURSOR'   "WA_UPLOAD-NEWKO.
                              'RF05A-NEWKO'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '/00'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-WRBTR'   WA_UPLOAD2-WRBTR.
*                              '1000'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-MWSKZ'   WA_UPLOAD2-MWSKZ.
*                              'V0'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-BUPLA'   WA_UPLOAD2-BUPLA.
*                              'HO01'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-ZUONR'   WA_UPLOAD2-ZUONR.
**                              '123456'.
**perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-SGTXT'
**                              'Test'.



LV_COUNT = LV_COUNT + 1.
READ TABLE IT_UPLOAD2 ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<FS_TEMP>) INDEX LV_COUNT.

IF SY-SUBRC = 0 AND <FS_TEMP>-SO_NO EQ WA_UPLOAD2-SO_NO.


perform bdc_field       using 'RF05A-NEWBS'  <FS_TEMP>-NEWBS.
*                              '50'.
perform bdc_field       using 'RF05A-NEWKO'  <FS_TEMP>-NEWKO.
*                              '40400650'.

perform bdc_dynpro      using 'SAPLKACB' '0002'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_CURSOR'   "WA_UPLOAD-PRCTR.
                              'COBL-PRCTR'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '=ENTE'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-GSBER'   WA_UPLOAD2-GSBER.
*                              'ho01'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-KOSTL'   WA_UPLOAD2-KOSTL.
*                              '50078867'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-AUFNR'   WA_UPLOAD2-AUFNR.
*                              '000002113431'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-PRCTR'   WA_UPLOAD2-PRCTR.
*                              '5001cp05'.


ELSE.

perform bdc_dynpro      using 'SAPLKACB' '0002'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_CURSOR'    "WA_UPLOAD-PRCTR.
                              'COBL-PRCTR'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '=ENTE'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-GSBER'   WA_UPLOAD2-GSBER.
*                              'ho01'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-KOSTL'   WA_UPLOAD2-KOSTL.

perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-AUFNR'   WA_UPLOAD2-AUFNR.
*                              '000002113431'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-PRCTR'   WA_UPLOAD2-PRCTR.
*                              '5001cp05'.


perform bdc_dynpro      using 'SAPMF05A' '0300'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_CURSOR'   "WA_UPLOAD-WRBTR.
                              'BSEG-WRBTR'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '=BU'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-WRBTR'   WA_UPLOAD2-WRBTR.
*                              '1,000.00'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-MWSKZ'   WA_UPLOAD2-MWSKZ.
*                              'V0'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-BUPLA'   WA_UPLOAD2-BUPLA.
**                              'HO01'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-ZUONR'   WA_UPLOAD2-ZUONR.
**                              '123456'.
**perform bdc_field       using 'BSEG-SGTXT'
**                              'Test'.
perform bdc_field       using 'DKACB-FMORE'
                              'X'.

perform bdc_dynpro      using 'SAPLKACB' '0002'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_CURSOR'   "WA_UPLOAD-GSBER.
                              'COBL-GSBER'.
perform bdc_field       using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '=ENTE'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-GSBER'   WA_UPLOAD2-GSBER.
*                              'HO01'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-KOSTL'   WA_UPLOAD2-KOSTL.
*                              '50010501'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-AUFNR'   WA_UPLOAD2-AUFNR.
*                              '2113431'.
perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-PRCTR'   WA_UPLOAD2-PRCTR.
*                              '5001CP05'.
*perform bdc_field       using 'COBL-SEGMENT' "WA_UPLOAD-SEGMENT.
*                              'IRON.
EXIT.
ENDIF.

CLEAR : WA_UPLOAD2.
ENDLOOP.

IF MODE_B = 'X'.
    CALL TRANSACTION 'FB05' USING IT_BDCDATA MODE 'N' UPDATE 'A' MESSAGES INTO IT_MSG.
ENDIF.

IF MODE_F = 'X'.
    CALL TRANSACTION 'FB05' USING IT_BDCDATA MODE 'A' UPDATE 'A' MESSAGES INTO IT_MSG.
ENDIF.

    REFRESH IT_BDCDATA.
    CLEAR : WA_UPLOAD.
ENDLOOP.

LOOP AT it_msg INTO wa_msg .

    CALL FUNCTION 'FORMAT_MESSAGE'
      EXPORTING
        id        = wa_msg-msgid
        lang      = sy-langu
        no        = wa_msg-msgnr
        v1        = wa_msg-msgv1
        v2        = wa_msg-msgv2
        v3        = wa_msg-msgv3
        v4        = wa_msg-msgv4
      IMPORTING
        msg       = l_message
      EXCEPTIONS
        not_found = 1
        OTHERS    = 2.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
*         WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
    ENDIF.

    WRITE:/ l_message .
  ENDLOOP .
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*        Start new screen                                              *
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM bdc_dynpro USING program dynpro.
  CLEAR wa_bdcdata.
  wa_bdcdata-program  = program.
  wa_bdcdata-dynpro   = dynpro.
  wa_bdcdata-dynbegin = 'X'.
  APPEND wa_bdcdata TO it_bdcdata.
ENDFORM.                    "

*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*        Insert field                                                  *
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM bdc_field USING fnam fval.
*  IF FVAL <> NODATA.
  CLEAR wa_bdcdata.
  wa_bdcdata-fnam = fnam.
  wa_bdcdata-fval = fval.
  SHIFT wa_bdcdata-fval LEFT DELETING LEADING SPACE.
  APPEND wa_bdcdata TO it_bdcdata.
*  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.

Rishabh.

entry.title: CDS View with aggregation
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to know if what I want to do is possible in CDS views without doing ABAP.

Is it possible to create a custom CDS view that have aggregation (sum of stock) already calculated in it. The goal would be to join this view to another one with aggregation.

We would like to do a report with available and reserved stock group by Material/Plant/Storage Location (basically MB52 with reserved)

First try, I create a Custom view on I_MATERIALSTOCK_2 with Material/Plant/Storage as Key and configured the Stock as sum with Element Properties.
Then, I create another custom view with I_ReservationDocumentItem similarly.
Then, I join those two view on the keys Material/Plant/Storage and create an analytical query on this view. This gives wrong values because each stock is multiplied by the number of lines from the other view.

Second try, I wanted to join the C_MaterialStockActual view to I_ReservationDocumentItem. But this fails as I cannot use C_MaterialStockActual in another CDS View.

Any guidance or feedback is appreciated.

Julien

entry.title: Re: Condition Contract for Sales - Manual Delta Accruals
entry.content:
Hello Michelle,

Like Gauthier said, in order to justify the open accrual balance you are bringing in as part of conversion, you need to convert the corresponding business volume (historical sales) from your legacy system. You can do that using the general billing interface, or BAPI BAPI_BILLINGDOC_CREATEMULTIPLE. This BAPI creates billing documents without preceding transactions. To justify the unplanned accrual balance, you need to use condition types REBD - business volume delta or REJ1 - rebate adjustment to match up the open accrual balance with your business volume.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SFEC Time Off - Handle contract-end-date with FTE and...
entry.content:
Hi,

We implemented a similar requirement for a Core Engineering consulting organization where there was a lot of hiring of Contractual employees, fixed term contractual employees and Consultants (also on contract)

The following breakdown was provided along with effort hours

Option - 1: 

1.Creation of a new time type - 8 hours

2.Create the necessary take rules like avoid backdated applications before a specific date and other validations - 12 hours

3.Create a field on job information and handle it's permissions- 2 hours

4.Create a new event reason under data change for job information and handle it's permissions - 4 hours

5.Create a business rule on job information that will auto populate the field created with the required accrual amount or zero - 25 hours

6.Create an off cycle event process or integration on Integration center that will upsert the record on employees job information with event date as contract end date and event as data change as created in point 4 at start of the calendar year - 20 hours

7. Creation of hire rule - 8 hours

8.Creation of accrual rule - 15 hours

9.Creation of PEP rule  - 10 hours

10.Internal testing, documentation and fixes - 40 hours

11.Migration and Sanity check  - 30 hours.

 

Total is coming out to be 174 effort hours (Approx, 22 working days)

 

Limitaton : The above mentioned solution will not fullfil the requirement of after CED is extended, there is another entitlement generated with value as 11 days and ONLY valid from 1st Apr - 31 Dec

Instead, after CED is extended, it will re-generate for WHOLE year as 14 days.

 

 

Option 2: This will work in a similar way like option 1, however to dynamically change the entitlement validity a CPI integration will be required along with the modifications and further complexity in the accrual rules.

This will easily add approximately 250 efforts for the interface creation, rules creation and testing with the interface for various scenarios.

Total effort hours : 424 (approx - 53 mandays)

entry.title: SAP Signavio is a leader in the SPARK Matrix™ report for Process Mining solutions.
entry.content:
SAP Signavio have again been named a Leader in the SPARK Matrix™ report for Process Mining Solutions and have received the highest rating for Technology Excellence.

SAP Signavio scores the highest for Technology Excellence

The QKS Group SPARK Matrix™ offers an in-depth analysis of global market dynamics, major trends, vendor landscapes, and competitive positioning. 22 leading process mining vendors have been assessed against several parameters within the Technology Excellence and Customer Impact dimensions.

According to QKS Group, “Process mining helps organizations to analyze, identify, automate, and monitor event logs and real-time systems data to enhance end-to-end operational business processes, where it includes a dynamic set of tools to capture data across an organization's IT systems and varied data sources. This helps organizations to identify gaps in business processes and solve issues that could impact the performance of these business processes.”

SAP Signavio have emerged as a leader in the report, ranking highest in technology excellence with their ability to deliver:

 

  • A single-vendor solution approach that integrates process discovery, modeling, end-to-end process mining, and monitoring capabilities, helping users obtain full enterprise observability and seamlessly manage complex processes 
  • A highly flexible process discovery solution with a comprehensive library of process widgets
  • A wide range of pre-built value accelerators for SAP and non-SAP applications and the plug and gain approach speeding up time-to-value 
  • An emphasis on AI-driven process discovery, advanced simulation for optimization, and real-time monitoring
  • Experience-driven process mining
  • High scalability that supports large-scale data models while maintaining robust security and governance

According to Pranjal Singh, Principal Industry Analyst: “The SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite offers 360 degrees view of processes, multi-level analysis, as well as end-to-end traceability enabling full enterprise observability. (…) The strategy emphasizes AI-driven process discovery, advanced simulation for optimization, and real-time monitoring. SAP Signavio enhances user accessibility with industry-specific solutions and seamless integration with SAP's portfolio, enabling swift data loading without extensive preparation. The platform's scalability, powered by AWS infrastructure, supports large-scale data models while maintaining robust security and governance. SAP Signavio's Value Accelerators offer pre-built content, best practices, and tailored guidance, facilitating accelerated process transformation. (..) SAP Signavio's user success strategy leverages SAP's global support network and Signavio's process management expertise.”

Alessandro Manzi, Head of Product Management at SAP Signavio, notes: “We are proud to be ranked as a leader and to excel on the technology dimension. The study perfectly underscores our dedication to empowering customers with comprehensive enterprise observability, actionable recommendations, and swift value realization. We stand out for technology excellence through a unique blend of human-driven and AI-assisted mining capabilities, seamlessly integrated within an end-to-end process transformation suite and backed by a pioneering innovation strategy.

We remain committed to investing in AI and AI agents and intensifying our focus on value-centric transformation. To this end, we are further extending the recently released SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager capabilities through advanced automated discovery, stronger value monitoring, and objective-based frameworks”.

Download your complimentary copy now, to:   

  • Gain insights on the process mining market trends and vendors from a trusted global analyst firm  
  • Learn why QKS Group names SAP Signavio as a Leader  
  • Discover the strengths that led to SAP Signavio solutions’ highest ratings for technology excellence

Get the report >>

The publication of the SPARK Matrix™: Process Mining, 2024 report on November 28th, follows the release of the SPARK Matrix™: Digital Twins of an Organization earlier this year, which classifies SAP Signavio as top leader. Learn more.

entry.title: Error generate maintenance license 'You do not have a maintenance contract for this installation.'
entry.content:
Customer get error 'You do not have a maintenance contract for this installation. Details in KBA. ' when generate license key.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Error generate maintenance license 'You do not have a...
entry.content:
KBA2436412 - License Keys application shows message: You do not have a maintenance contract for this installation - SAP for Me - SAP for Me:

To fix the issue with the maintenance contract/agreement, you must e-mail SAP Order Management (contracts) at sapcontractcenter@sap.com and ask them to fix the expired maintenance contract for the installation(s). Be sure to include the following:

  • Customer number
  • Customer name
  • Product/version
  • Installation number(s) with expired maintenance

See Also:

  • To view installations with expired maintenance, open Installation Management and look for installation numbers with "Expired" in the maintenance column.
  • To renew maintenance certificates, a valid support maintenance contract is required, otherwise you cannot renew maintenance certificates (see KBA 1898812).
  • When the "maintenance contract" message is displayed while creating license keys, you can continue and complete the license key request process -- but the license keys may be temporary instead of permanent*.
    *If your customer number has a valid license agreement for the product that you are requesting a license key for, this message does not impact you.
  • When your Customer Number does not have a valid support maintenance contract in place, many SAP support services such as maintenance certificates, opening support cases, etc., may not be available to you.

entry.title: Re: Check compatibility pack usage in SAP S/4HANA with SAP EarlyWatch Alert
entry.content:
When you compare the outputs of the Readiness Check with the SAP EarlyWatch Alert chapter on Compatibility Package usage, there will be some differences. Here is a summary why: 

  1. The Readiness Check always evaluates the compatibility scope based on the target release for an upgrade. Some Compatibility Package items have been modernised in higher S/4HANA releases, meaning they become part of the perpetual scope when upgraded to the appropriate release, and thus, are no longer listed. However, for a status check on the current release, this might appear as a false negative if customers have not performed the upgrade by the end of 2025. Therefore, we also check actual usage and display these items in the EarlyWatch Alert (EWA) if they are used.
  2. In the Readiness Check and the simplification items used for this process, customization and table contents are also considered for relevance checks, which are not fully implemented in the EWA framework. This can result in false positives in the Readiness Check if historical data or customizations exist for applications that are no longer in use or have already been converted.
  3. The simplification items also include usage checks for transactions and reports. These checks are based on the complete ST03N period, up to a maximum of 12 months. In the EWA, we check up to a maximum of 3 months because we aim to avoid sending unnecessary alerts to customers who have already switched to successor applications. Additionally, we do not have Compatibility Package items that are executed annually or semi-annually, so the EWA does not need these long-term checks. This approach can lead to false positives in the simplification item check of the Readiness Check.

2)When I deploy the application in SAP -on premise system 2023 and clicked on the below tile
